decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip center. I will increase the spreader conductivity by a factor of 1.5, which is within the allowed single-step ratio and bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the localized hot spot under the chip to meet the 85°C constraint.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if conductivity becomes too high relative to the base plate extraction capacity
Further iterations may require adjusting base_material.conductivity or spreader geometry if this single change is insufficient
